headline: DeepMind/Google moves from 58.4 to 55.6 (-2.8) — sub-threshold confirmation. New in-window signal: 100+ DeepMind-specific researchers signed an internal letter demanding no DeepMind research or models be used for weapons development or autonomous targeting, distinct from and more targeted than the broader 1,000-employee Google letter previously scored. Andreas Kirsch publicly stated he was 'incredibly ashamed.' Leadership has not altered deal terms despite escalation. Compound INT and ACT pressure; BND modest pressure on scope-clarity. Composite -2.8 is sub-threshold and does not cross the Functional/Developing boundary at 40. Confirmation-only with minor dimension adjustments documenting in-window evidence.
key_evidence: 100+ DeepMind researchers signed a separate internal letter — distinct from broader 1,000-employee Google letter — demanding that no DeepMind research or models be used for weapons development or autonomous targeting.
Andreas Kirsch (Senior Research Scientist, Google DeepMind) publicly stated 'I personally feel incredibly ashamed right now to be Senior Research Scientist at Google DeepMind' on X.

Open letter to CEO Pichai from 600+ Google employees (some sources cite ~1,000) including 20+ directors, senior directors, and VPs warned classified military AI work could cause 'irreparable damage to Google's reputation, business, and role in the world.'
Pentagon classified deal terms permit use of Gemini AI on classified networks for 'any lawful government purpose' — broader than narrow-use military integration.
Google leadership has not altered deal terms within window despite documented internal opposition (Fortune May 4).

evidence.source: Transformer News (DeepMind employees made their opposition clear)
evidence.finding: Internal DeepMind-specific letter from 100+ researchers — distinct, more targeted protest than broader Google employee letter — demanding no DeepMind research or models be used for weapons development or autonomous targeting.
Andreas Kirsch (DeepMind senior research scientist) publicly stated 'incredibly ashamed' to be working at Google DeepMind following deal confirmation.

580+ Google employees including DeepMind researchers signed open letter to Pichai urging refusal of classified Pentagon AI deal; warning of 'irreparable damage to Google's reputation, business, and role in the world.'
Employee organizing broader than Project Maven precedent but company leadership has not altered deal terms within window. Power asymmetry vs. 2018 Maven crisis noted.

scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.2 unchanged. Internal employee experience documented but no structural empathy-process change.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.3 (-0.1). AC1 Responsiveness: leadership has not altered course in response to ~1,000 internal voices flagging foreseeable harm pathways. Anchor moves modestly toward 3.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.1 (-0.1). B3 Scope Clarity: 'any lawful governmental purpose' classified terms expand scope without corresponding model-use oversight; DeepMind cannot verify queries, outputs, or operational decisions made with deployed models. Anchor moves slightly downward.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.0 unchanged. AB2 Correction Willingness pressure exists but no formal harm acknowledgment yet — held pending company response.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.6 unchanged. Same-week drone-swarm exit (positive signal already scored) offsets pressure on systemic dimensions.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.8 (-0.2). I3 Internal Consistency: a 100+ DeepMind-specific letter combined with the broader 1,000-employee Google letter is a structural signal that internal stakeholders no longer experience the same value-orientation as external communications. I4 Values Alignment: 'Don't be evil' founding-era values disconnected from current decisions. Anchor moves from 3 toward 2.5.

math_check.validator_check: Sub-threshold delta (-2.8); within validator 2.0pt cycle ceiling at the dimension level; Functional band sustained.

negativeSignals: 100+ DeepMind-specific researcher letter; Andreas Kirsch public 'incredibly ashamed' statement; Google leadership unchanged after escalation; Pentagon 'any lawful governmental purpose' terms.
positiveSignals: Same-week drone-swarm contract exit (already scored); employees retain organizing capacity and public voice — internal-pressure infrastructure intact.
sector_context: Across the AI Labs sector, DeepMind/Google is the only major lab with confirmed >100-person internal-research-staff protest specific to its weapons-AI posture within window. This contrasts with Anthropic's continued Pentagon-cohort exclusion and complements OpenAI's separate trial-disclosure pressure.
open_actions_resolved: Pending 2026-04-30 proposal resolved with this assessment
100+ DeepMind-specific letter scored under INT and ACT
Andreas Kirsch public statement scored under INT
open_actions_carried: Monitor for any Google leadership response to internal letter — none in-window as of scan
May 19 DC Circuit oral arguments may produce contrastive industry-context evidence
Composite at 55.6 — within 5.6 points of 60-band boundary (now further away than prior cycle)
